info.title: Loops OpenAPI Spec
info.description: This is the OpenAPI Spec for the [Loops API](https://loops.so/docs/api).

get.description: Test your API key and get team context.

post.description: Add a contact to your audience.

requestBody.description: You can add custom contact properties as keys in this request (of type `string`, `number`, `boolean` or `date`. [See available date formats](https://loops.so/docs/contacts/properties#dates)). Make sure to create the properties in Loops before using them in API calls.

put.description:
Update a contact by `email` or `userId`. You must provide one of these parameters.

If you want to update a contact's email address, the contact will first need a `userId` value. You can then make a request containing the `userId` field along with an updated email address.

This endpoint will create a contact if a matching contact does not already exist in your audience.

Provide either `email` or `userId` to identify the contact you want to update. If both are provided, the system will look for a contact with either a matching `email` or `userId` value. If a contact is found for one of the values (e.g. `email`), the other value (e.g. `userId`) will be updated. If a contact is not found, a new contact will be created using both `email` and `userId` values.

requestBody.description: You can add custom contact properties as keys in this request (of type `string`, `number`, `boolean` or `date`. [See available date formats](https://loops.so/docs/contacts/properties#dates)). Make sure to create the properties in Loops before using them in API calls.

get.description: Search for a contact by `email` or `userId`. Only one parameter is allowed.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address (URI-encoded)

200.description: List of contacts (or an empty array if no contact was found). Contact objects will include any custom properties.

post.description: Delete a contact by `email` or `userId`.

400.description: Bad request (e.g. `email` and `userId` are both provided).

get.summary: Get suppression status for a contact
get.description: Retrieve suppression status and removal quota for a contact by `email` or `userId`. Include only one query parameter.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address (URI-encoded)

delete.summary: Remove a contact from suppression list
delete.description: Remove a suppressed contact from the suppression list by `email` or `userId`. Include only one query parameter.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address (URI-encoded)

400.description: Bad request (e.g. contact is not suppressed).

post.description: Create a custom contact property.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of your account's contact properties.
Use the `list` parameter to query "all" or "custom" properties.

parameters.name: list
parameters.description: `all` (default) or `custom` (only custom contact properties)

get.description:
Retrieve a list of Loops' dedicated sending IP addresses.

This endpoint is provided for the rare instances where you may need to whitelist our sending IPs. Please note that this list is subject to change and will not include shared IPs used for sending mail.

Unless you are sure you need this and are comfortable watching for changes, we strongly recommend you _do not_ whitelist these IPs.
Retrieve a list of your account's mailing lists.

post.description: Send events to trigger workflows.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description:
Include a unique ID for this request (maximum 100 characters) to avoid duplicate emails.

The value should be a string of up to 100 characters and should be unique for each request. We recommend using V4 UUIDs or some other method with enough guaranteed entropy to avoid collisions during a 24 hour window.

This endpoint will return a `409 Conflict` response if the idempotency key has been used in the previous 24 hours.

requestBody.description:
Provide either `email` or `userId` to identify the contact ([read more](https://loops.so/docs/api-reference/send-event#body)).
Event properties will be available in emails sent by this event. Values of properties can be of type `string`, `number`, `boolean` or `date` ([see allowed date formats](https://loops.so/docs/events/properties#important-information-about-event-properties)).
Make sure to create the properties in Loops before using them in API calls.
You can add contact properties as keys in this request (of type `string`, `number`, `boolean` or `date` ([see available date formats](https://loops.so/docs/contacts/properties#dates))).

post.description:
Send a transactional email to a contact.
Please [email us](mailto:help@loops.so) to enable attachments on your account before using them with the API. To set dynamic Subject, From, Reply to, CC, BCC email header fields, add data variables to those fields in the editor, then include data for each variable in the API request. Read our transactional email guide for more details.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description:
Include a unique ID for this request (maximum 100 characters) to avoid duplicate emails.

The value should be a string of up to 100 characters and should be unique for each request. We recommend using V4 UUIDs or some other method with enough guaranteed entropy to avoid collisions during a 24 hour window.

This endpoint will return a `409 Conflict` response if the idempotency key has been used in the previous 24 hours.

400.description: Bad request (e.g. transactional email is not published).

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of transactional emails, most recently created first.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

post.description: Create a new transactional email. An empty draft email message is created automatically and its `draftEmailMessageId` is returned. Use the `POST /v1/email-messages/{emailMessageId}` endpoint along with the returned `draftEmailMessageContentRevisionId` to set subject, sender, preview text, and LMX content, then call `POST /v1/transactional-emails/{transactionalId}/publish` to publish the draft.

400.description: Invalid request body or no sending domain configured.

get.description: Retrieve a single transactional email by ID.

post.description: Update a transactional email by ID.

parameters.name: transactionalId
parameters.description: The ID of the transactional email.

post.description: Ensure the transactional email has a draft email message. If a draft already exists it is returned unchanged; otherwise a new empty draft is created (seeded from the most recent published version when present). Use `POST /v1/email-messages/{emailMessageId}` to edit the draft's content.

400.description: Invalid `transactionalId` or no sending domain configured.

parameters.name: transactionalId
parameters.description: The ID of the transactional email.

post.description: Publish the transactional email's current draft email message. The draft becomes the published version and the draft is cleared.

422.description: Draft failed validation, sending domain is not verified, or content was flagged as unsafe.

parameters.name: transactionalId
parameters.description: The ID of the transactional email.

get.description: Retrieve a single theme by ID.

post.description: "Update a theme's name and/or styles. When `styles` change, the update cascades to every email using this theme, and `affectedEmailCount` in the response reports how many emails were affected. Manual style edits made on individual emails are preserved: the cascade only changes properties an email has not overridden. A per-email override is removed only when it becomes identical to the theme's new value, after which that email follows the theme for that property."

parameters.name: themeId
parameters.description: The ID of the theme.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of email themes, most recently created first.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

post.description: Create a new email theme for use across Loops emails.

get.description: Retrieve a single component by ID.

post.description: Update a component's name and/or body. When the `lmx` body changes, the update cascades to every email using this component, and `affectedEmailCount` reports how many were affected. A change that would introduce a dynamic variable an email using the component cannot use is rejected.

413.description: LMX body exceeds the size limit.

422.description: Invalid LMX, or a body change that would break emails using the component.

parameters.name: componentId
parameters.description: The ID of the component.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of email components.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

post.description: Create a new email component from an LMX body.

413.description: LMX body exceeds the size limit.

get.description: Retrieve a single audience segment by ID.

parameters.name: audienceSegmentId
parameters.description: The ID of the audience segment.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of audience segments, most recently created first.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

post.description: Create a new audience segment.

400.description: Invalid request body, a name already used by another segment, or a filter with too many conditions.

404.description: The filter references a campaign, workflow or workflow email that does not exist.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of campaigns.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

post.description: Create a new draft campaign. An empty email message is created automatically and its `emailMessageId` is returned. Use the `/email-messages/{emailMessageId}` endpoint to set subject, sender, preview text, and LMX content. The audience (mailing list, segment, or filter), group, and scheduling can be set on create or later via update.

400.description: Invalid request body, campaign group not found, or no sending domain configured.

404.description: Referenced mailing list or audience segment not found.

get.description: Retrieve a single campaign by ID.

post.description: Update a draft campaign's name, group, audience (mailing list, segment, or filter), or scheduling. At least one field must be provided. Campaigns can only be updated while in draft status.

400.description: Invalid request body or campaign group not found.

404.description: Campaign, mailing list, or audience segment not found.

409.description: Campaign is not in draft status.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign.

get.description: Retrieve an email message, including its compiled LMX content.

400.description: Invalid `emailMessageId` or no sending domain configured.

409.description: Email message uses MJML format or content cannot be parsed.

post.description: Update fields on an email message (subject, preview text, sender, LMX content). The campaign must be in draft status. Supply `expectedRevisionId` matching the current `contentRevisionId` — the server rejects mismatched revisions with 409.

409.description: Campaign is not in draft status, `contentRevisionId` is stale, content cannot be parsed, or email message uses MJML format.

parameters.name: emailMessageId
parameters.description: The ID of the email message.

post.summary: Send a preview of an email message
post.description: Send a test preview of an email message to one or more addresses. The accepted variable fields depend on the parent's type - campaign previews accept `contactProperties`, workflow previews accept `contactProperties` and `eventProperties`, and transactional previews accept `dataVariables`. Supplying a field the parent cannot reference is rejected with 400.

400.description: Invalid request body, or a variable field the parent cannot reference.

429.description: The daily preview limit was reached.

parameters.name: emailMessageId
parameters.description: The ID of the email message.

get.summary: Run Guardian checks on an email message
get.description: Validate an email message's content against Guardian rules and return any errors and warnings. Errors must be resolved before the email can be published, warnings are advisory.

parameters.name: emailMessageId
parameters.description: The ID of the email message.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of workflows.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

post.description: Create a draft workflow with a blank trigger and exit node.

500.description: Workflow creation is unavailable for this team.

get.description: Retrieve a workflow graph with node type names, connections, and selected display fields.

post.description: Update a workflow's display properties. At least one property must be provided. To change the workflow's mailing list, use the `POST /v1/workflows/{workflowId}/mailing-list` endpoint instead.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow.

post.description: "Dry run or apply a workflow mailing list change. If queued contacts would be removed from the workflow due to the change of mailing list, Loops returns with `\"status\": \"queuedContactsFound\"` instead of applying the change. Retry with `queuedContactPolicy: \"discard\"` to apply the change and discard those contacts."

200.description: Mailing-list dry run, queued-contact warning, or update result.

400.description: Invalid `workflowId`, request body, mailing list, or workflow state.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow.

post.description:
"Create a new default workflow node and return it with the latest workflow.

Choose where the node goes with `insertMode`: `between` places it between an existing `fromNodeId` -> `toNodeId` connection, and `before` places it before `beforeNodeId`.

New nodes start with default settings; update the node after creation to configure it. Branch nodes create their default paths too: `BranchNode` creates two `AudienceFilter` children, and `ExperimentBranchNode` creates two regular `VariantNode` children plus one control `VariantNode`. Public workflows can have up to 300 nodes. Generated children count toward that limit, so a normal create adds 1 node, `BranchNode` adds 3, and `ExperimentBranchNode` adds 4. To add a new branch or experiment branch, use the `POST /v1/workflows/{workflowId}/nodes/{nodeId}/add-branch` endpoint instead."

400.description: Invalid request. Details will be shown in `message`.

404.description: Workflow or workflow node not found.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow.

post.description:
Add a branch and a child node under an existing Branch or Experiment node. Returns the created child node plus the latest workflow.

- Adding a branch to a `BranchNode` creates one `AudienceFilter` child node.
- Adding a branch to an `ExperimentBranchNode` creates one `VariantNode` child node.

This endpoint does not accept node configuration fields; update the child node with `POST /v1/workflows/{workflowId}/nodes/{nodeId}` after creation. Public workflows are limited to 300 nodes, and this endpoint adds 1 node.

400.description: Invalid `workflowId`, `nodeId`, request body, branch target, workflow state, or workflow node cap.

404.description: Workflow or workflow node not found.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: The ID of the BranchNode or ExperimentBranchNode that should receive one new child.

get.description: Retrieve detailed data for a single workflow node.

404.description: Workflow or workflow node not found.

post.description: Update workflow-node-owned fields for a single node. Shared resources such as email messages and audience segments should be updated through their own APIs.

400.description: Invalid `workflowId`, `nodeId`, request body, or node update.

404.description: Workflow or workflow node not found.

501.description: This node update is not implemented.

delete.description: "Delete a single workflow node. If contacts are queued at the node, Loops returns with `\"status\": \"queuedContactsFound\"` instead of deleting. Retry with `queuedContactPolicy: \"discard\"` to delete the node and discard those queued contacts."

200.description: Delete dry run, queued-contact warning, or deletion result.

400.description: Invalid `workflowId`, `nodeId`, request body, or delete target.

404.description: Workflow or workflow node not found.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow node.

delete.description: "Delete a node and its downstream subtree. If contacts are queued at any node that would be deleted, Loops returns with `\"status\": \"queuedContactsFound\"` instead of deleting. Retry with `queuedContactPolicy: \"discard\"` to delete the nodes and discard those queued contacts."

200.description: Delete dry run, queued-contact warning, or deletion result.

400.description: Invalid `workflowId`, `nodeId`, request body, or delete target.

404.description: Workflow or workflow node not found.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: The root node ID of the subtree to delete.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of event patterns available to workflow event trigger nodes.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

get.summary: Get an event pattern by name
get.description: Retrieve event pattern details by event name for a workflow event trigger. Event names are case-sensitive, so `PaymentReceived` and `paymentReceived` are different events.

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.description: The exact event name. Event names are case-sensitive and should be URL-encoded if they contain special characters.

get.summary: Get an event pattern by ID
get.description: Retrieve event pattern details for a workflow event trigger.

parameters.name: eventPatternId
parameters.description: The ID of the event pattern.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of campaign groups, most recently created first.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

post.description: Create a new campaign group.

400.description: Invalid request body or reserved group name.

get.description: Retrieve a single campaign group by ID.

post.description: Update a campaign group's name or description. At least one field must be provided. The reserved "Unsorted" group cannot be edited.

400.description: Invalid request body, reserved group name, or the Unsorted group cannot be edited.

parameters.name: campaignGroupId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign group.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of transactional groups, most recently created first.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

post.description: Create a new transactional group.

400.description: Invalid request body or reserved group name.

get.description: Retrieve a single transactional group by ID.

post.description: Update a transactional group's name or description. At least one field must be provided. The reserved "Unsorted" group cannot be edited.

400.description: Invalid request body, reserved group name, or the Unsorted group cannot be edited.

parameters.name: transactionalGroupId
parameters.description: The ID of the transactional group.

post.description: Request a pre-signed URL to upload an image asset. Upload the file with an HTTP `PUT` to the returned `presignedUrl` (sending the same `Content-Type` and `Content-Length`), then call `/uploads/{emailAssetId}/complete` to finalize the asset.

413.description: Upload exceeds the maximum allowed size.

post.description: Finalize an image upload after the file has been uploaded to the pre-signed URL. Returns the public URL of the uploaded image asset.

400.description: Upload id is missing or the uploaded file has an unsupported content type.

parameters.name: emailAssetId
parameters.description: The `emailAssetId` returned when the upload was created via `POST /v1/uploads`.

source.description: The source the contact was created from.

subscribed.description: Whether the contact will receive campaign and workflow emails.

source.description: A custom source value to replace the default “API”.

subscribed.description: Whether the contact will receive campaign and workflow emails. All new contacts are subscribed by default.

userId.description: A unique user ID (for example, from an external application).

source.description: A custom source value to replace the default “API”.

subscribed.description: Whether the contact will receive campaign and workflow emails. All new contacts are subscribed by default.

userId.description: A unique user ID (for example, from an external application).

source.description: A custom source value to replace the default “API”.

subscribed.description: Whether the contact will receive campaign and workflow emails. All new contacts are subscribed by default.

userId.description: A unique user ID (for example, from an external application).

success.description: Whether the suppression removal was successful.

limit.description: The number of suppression removals allowed in a rolling 30 day period.

remaining.description: The remaining number of suppression removals left in the current 30 day period.

name.description: The name of the property. This should be in camelCase, like `planName` or `importDate`.

ContactPropertyCreateRequest.description: There are a few [reserved names](https://loops.so/docs/contacts/properties#reserved-names) that you cannot use for contact properties.

email.description: The contact's email address. **Required if `userId` is not provided.**

userId.description: The contact's unique user ID. **Required if `email` is not provided.**

transactionalId.description: The ID of the transactional email to send.

addToAudience.description: If `true`, a contact will be created in your audience using the `email` value (if a matching contact doesn't already exist).

attachments.description: A list containing file objects to be sent along with an email message. Attachments must be enabled by Loops support before they can be used with the API.

id.description: The ID of the transactional email.

name.description: The name of the transactional email.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time the transactional email was last updated in ISO 8601 format.

dataVariables.description: The data variables used by the transactional email.

id.description: The ID of the transactional email.

name.description: The name of the transactional email.

draftEmailMessageId.description: The ID of the draft email message. `null` if there is no draft version.

publishedEmailMessageId.description: The ID of the published email message. `null` if there is no published version.

transactionalGroupId.description: The ID of the group this transactional email belongs to.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the transactional email was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the transactional email was last updated.

dataVariables.description: Data variable names used by the published email. Empty for unpublished transactional emails.

id.description: The ID of the transactional email.

name.description: The name of the transactional email.

draftEmailMessageId.description: The ID of the draft email message.

draftEmailMessageContentRevisionId.description: The `contentRevisionId` of the draft email message. Pass this as `expectedRevisionId` on your first update via `POST /v1/email-messages/{emailMessageId}`.

publishedEmailMessageId.description: The ID of the published email message. `null` if there is no published version.

transactionalGroupId.description: The ID of the group this transactional email belongs to. Returned when creating a transactional email.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the transactional email was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the transactional email was last updated.

dataVariables.description: Data variable names used by the published email. Empty for unpublished transactional emails.

name.description: The name of the transactional email.

transactionalGroupId.description: The ID of the group to add this transactional email to. Defaults to the team's default group when omitted.
The ID of the group to move this transactional email to.

UpdateTransactionalRequest.description: At least one field must be provided.

key.description: The key of the contact property.

label.description: The human-friendly label for this property.

id.description: The ID of the mailing list.

name.description: The name of the mailing list.

description.description: The description of the mailing list. `null` if no description is set.

isPublic.description: Whether the mailing list is public (`true`) or private (`false`).

MailingListSubscriptions.description:
Manage mailing list subscriptions.

Include key-value pairs of mailing list IDs and a `boolean` denoting if the contact should be added (`true`) or removed (`false`) from the list.

isDefault.description: Whether this theme is the team's default.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the theme was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the theme was last updated.

ThemeStyles.description: Flat map of style attributes, matching the attribute names accepted by the LMX `<Style />` tag.

isDefault.description: Whether this theme is the team's default.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the theme was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the theme was last updated.

UpdateThemeBody.description: At least one of `name` or `styles` must be provided.

isDefault.description: Whether this theme is the team's default.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the theme was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the theme was last updated.

affectedEmailCount.description: The number of emails using this theme that are affected by the style change. `0` when only the name changed.

lmx.description: The component body serialized as LMX.
The component body serialized as LMX.

invalidTags.description: The dynamic variables that the change would push into an email that cannot use them. Present only when the update was rejected for that reason.

lmx.description: The component body as an LMX string.
The component body as an LMX string.

UpdateComponentBody.description: At least one of `name` or `lmx` must be provided.

lmx.description: The component body serialized as LMX.

affectedEmailCount.description: The number of emails using this component that were updated by the body change. `0` when only the name changed.

id.description: The ID of the audience segment.

name.description: The name of the audience segment.

description.description: An optional description of the audience segment.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the audience segment was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the audience segment was last updated.

name.description: The name of the audience segment. Must be unique within the team.

description.description: An optional description of the audience segment.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 send time. Null when the method is `now`.

CampaignScheduling.description: When the campaign is scheduled to send.

CampaignSchedulingRequest.description: When the campaign should send. `timestamp` is required and must be in the future when `method` is `schedule`, and must be omitted when `method` is `now`.

AudienceFilter.description: A tree of audience conditions combined with `match`.

AudienceFilterInRequest.description: A tree of audience conditions combined with `match`. Setting this without also providing `audienceSegmentId` clears any existing `audienceSegmentId`. When both are provided, this filter is applied on top of the segment's filter.

PropertyCondition.description: Matches contacts by a property value.

OptInCondition.description: Matches contacts by mailing-list opt-in status.

id.description: The ID of the campaign, workflow, or workflow email.

ActivityCondition.description: Matches contacts by their activity on a campaign or workflow.

campaignGroupId.description: The ID of the group to add this campaign to. Defaults to the team's default group when omitted.

mailingListId.description: The ID of the mailing list to send to.

audienceSegmentId.description: The ID of an audience segment. Setting this without also providing `audienceFilter` clears any existing `audienceFilter`. If both are provided, the filter is applied on top of the segment's filter.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the campaign was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the campaign was last updated.

emailMessageId.description: The ID of the empty email message created for this campaign. Use `POST /v1/email-messages/{emailMessageId}` to set its fields and LMX content.

emailMessageContentRevisionId.description: The `contentRevisionId` of the newly created email message. Pass this as `expectedRevisionId` on your first update.

campaignGroupId.description: The ID of the campaign group this campaign belongs to, if set.

mailingListId.description: The ID of the mailing list this campaign sends to, if set.

audienceSegmentId.description: The ID of the audience segment this campaign targets, if set.

campaignGroupId.description: The ID of the group to move this campaign to.

mailingListId.description: The ID of the mailing list to send to.

audienceSegmentId.description: The ID of an audience segment. Setting this without also providing `audienceFilter` clears any existing `audienceFilter`. If both are provided, the filter is applied on top of the segment's filter.

UpdateCampaignRequest.description: At least one field must be provided.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the campaign was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the campaign was last updated.

campaignGroupId.description: The ID of the campaign group this campaign belongs to.

mailingListId.description: The ID of the mailing list this campaign sends to, if set.

audienceSegmentId.description: The ID of the audience segment this campaign targets, if set.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the workflow was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the workflow was last updated.

email.description: The contact's email address. **Required if `userId` is not provided.**

subscribed.description: Whether the contact will receive campaign and workflow emails. We recommend leaving this field out of your update requests unless you specifically want to unsubscribe (`false`) or re-subscribe (`true`) a contact. All new contacts are subscribed by default.

userId.description: The contact's unique user ID. **Required if `email` is not provided.**

NullableWorkflowRevisionId.description: The current workflow revision token. Pass the latest value as `expectedRevisionId` on the next workflow mutation. Will be `null` for workflows without a revision token yet.

WorkflowNextNodeIds.description: The IDs of the nodes that are downstream of this node.

WorkflowExpectedRevisionId.description: The workflow revision token returned by the latest workflow read or mutation. Older workflows may return `null` before their first revision-aware mutation; pass `null` back as `expectedRevisionId` in that case. If the token is stale, the API returns a `409 Conflict` error.

WorkflowQueuedContactPolicy.description: `fail` returns queued-contact impact instead of mutating. `discard` confirms that matching queued contacts should be discarded. Defaults to `fail` when omitted.

mailingListId.description: The ID of the mailing list the workflow sends to.

rootNodeId.description: The ID of the root node in the workflow graph.

UpdateWorkflowNodePayload.description: Node-type-specific fields to update. The allowed fields depend on the existing node type. Trigger node updates may include `typeName` when changing one trigger node type to another trigger node type.

WorkflowSignupTriggerPayload.description: Changes an existing trigger node to a signup trigger.

eventPatternId.description: The ID of the event pattern to trigger on. Use either `eventPatternId` or `eventName`, not both. Set to `null` to clear the event-pattern relationship.

eventName.description: The name of the event pattern to trigger on. Use this when you know the event name but not the internal event pattern ID. Use either `eventName` or `eventPatternId`, not both. Set to `null` to clear the event-pattern relationship.

reEligible.description: If `true`, the contacts will be able to enter this workflow every time the trigger is matched. If `false`, contacts will only ever enter this workflow once. Matches the "Trigger frequency" option in the UI.

WorkflowEventTriggerPayload.description: Updates an event trigger, or changes an existing trigger node to an event trigger. Assign the event pattern with either `eventPatternId` or `eventName`, not both. Set either field to `null` to clear the event-pattern relationship.

reEligible.description: If `true`, the contacts will be able to enter this workflow every time the trigger is matched. If `false`, contacts will only ever enter this workflow once. Matches the "Trigger frequency" option in the UI.

WorkflowContactPropertyTriggerPayload.description: Updates a contact-property trigger, or changes an existing trigger node to a contact-property trigger.

reEligible.description: If `true`, the contacts will be able to enter this workflow every time the trigger is matched. If `false`, contacts will only ever enter this workflow once. Matches the "Trigger frequency" option in the UI.

WorkflowAddToListTriggerPayload.description: Updates an add-to-list trigger, or changes an existing trigger node to an add-to-list trigger.

audienceSegmentId.description: The ID of an audience segment. Setting this without also providing `audienceFilter` clears any existing `audienceFilter`. If both are provided, the filter is applied on top of the segment's filter.

appliesDownstream.description: If `true`, the audience filter will apply to all downstream nodes. If `false`, the audience filter will only apply to the current node. Matches the "Filter scope" option in the UI.

amount.description: The amount of time to wait before triggering the next node. Set to `0` to move to the next node immediately.

WorkflowTimerActionPayload.description: Configuration for the timer action node.

samplingRate.description: The percentage of contacts that will be sent to variant branches, between `0` and `100`. The remaining percentage will be sent to the control branch. `100` sends all contacts to variant branches.

WorkflowExperimentBranchPayload.description: Configuration for the experiment branch node.

isControl.description: Use `true` to set this variant as the control of the experiment. This will set `false` on the existing control, if one exists. Experiments do not require a control variant.

CreateWorkflowNodeTypeName.description: Node types that can be created with the API. `*Trigger` nodes and `ExitAction` nodes cannot be created.

fromNodeId.description: The node to insert after. This node must currently point to `toNodeId`.

beforeNodeId.description: The node to insert before. The target must have at least one incoming parent and cannot be a trigger node.

CreateWorkflowNodeRequest.description: Create a new workflow node with an explicit `insertMode`. To configure the node after creation, use the `POST /v1/workflows/{workflowId}/nodes/{nodeId}` endpoint.

key.description: The camel-cased `key` of the contact property to query. The property must exist for the team and must be available for Contact Updated triggers.

WorkflowContactPropertyQuery.description: Define the contact property change that triggers the workflow. In update requests, `key` must resolve to an existing contact property that is available for Contact Updated triggers. Hidden or unsupported fields, such as `createdAt`, `notes`, and computed contact properties, are rejected.

WorkflowContactPropertyComparison.description: For Contact Updated triggers, the API validates `operator` against the selected contact property's type and the side of the comparison. The `was` comparison can use any operator supported by the selected property type. The `is` comparison uses the same operators, except number and boolean properties cannot use `empty`. String properties support `any`, `equal`, `not_equal`, `contains`, `not_contains`, `empty`, and `not_empty`. Number properties support `any`, `greater_than`, `less_than`, `numeric_equal`, `numeric_not_equal`, `empty`, and `not_empty`. Boolean properties support `any`, `true`, `false`, `empty`, and `not_empty`. Date properties support `any`, `empty`, `not_empty`, `after`, `before`, and `between`.

WorkflowTimerUnit.description: The unit of time for the timer action node. m = minutes, h = hours, d = days.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the group was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the group was last updated.

name.description: The group name. Cannot be the reserved name "Unsorted".

description.description: An optional description for the group.

name.description: The group name. Cannot be the reserved name "Unsorted".

UpdateGroupRequest.description: At least one field must be provided.

expectedRevisionId.description: The `contentRevisionId` you last fetched, or the `emailMessageContentRevisionId` you received when creating the campaign.

fromEmail.description: The email sender email address, without the team's sending domain.

replyToEmail.description: Reply-to email. Must be empty or a valid email address.

ccEmail.description: CC email address. Requires the team to have CC/BCC enabled.

bccEmail.description: BCC email address. Requires the team to have CC/BCC enabled.

languageCode.description: Language code for the email. Requires translation to be enabled for the team.

lmx.description: The email body serialized as LMX. Styles must be embedded in the LMX `<Style />` tag.

id.description: The ID of the email message.

campaignId.description: The campaign this email message belongs to. Present only when the message belongs to a campaign (mutually exclusive with `transactionalId`).

transactionalId.description: The transactional email this email message belongs to. Present only when the message belongs to a transactional email (mutually exclusive with `campaignId`).

fromEmail.description: The email sender email address, without the team's sending domain.

ccEmail.description: The email CC address. Only present when set.

bccEmail.description: The email BCC address. Only present when set.

lmx.description: The email body serialized as LMX.

contentRevisionId.description: The current content revision. Pass this as `expectedRevisionId` on your next update.

warnings.description: Non-fatal issues raised while compiling the submitted LMX. Only present on update responses when warnings were produced.

emails.description: One or more addresses to send the preview to.

id.description: The ID of the email message the preview was sent for.

errors.description: Validation errors. These must be resolved before the email can be published.

warnings.description: Validation warnings. These are advisory and do not block publishing.

errors.title: Missing button link
errors.description: Buttons won't work without href value

errors.title: Missing text link
errors.description: Links won't work without href value

title.description: A short summary of the rule.

description.description: A longer explanation of why the issue matters.

items.description: The specific elements that triggered the rule.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file to upload. Supported types are `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/gif` and `image/webp`.

contentLength.description: The size of the file in bytes. Must be a positive integer no greater than 4,000,000 bytes.

emailAssetId.description: The ID of the created asset. Pass this as `emailAssetId` to `POST /v1/uploads/{emailAssetId}/complete` once the file has been uploaded.

presignedUrl.description: The pre-signed URL to upload the file to with an HTTP `PUT` request. Send the same `Content-Type` and `Content-Length` used in the create request.

emailAssetId.description: The ID of the created asset.

finalUrl.description: The public URL of the uploaded asset.

maxUploads.description: The maximum number of uploads allowed per window.

windowHours.description: The number of hours in the upload limit window.

supportedContentTypes.description: Present when the request was rejected for an unsupported `contentType`. Lists the accepted MIME types.

maxBytes.description: Present when the upload exceeds the size limit. The maximum allowed size in bytes.

affectedEmailCount.description: The number of emails using this theme that are affected by the style change. `0` when only the name changed.
The number of emails using this component that were updated by the body change. `0` when only the name changed.

PropertyConditionValue.description: The comparison value. Omitted for value-less operators (e.g. `isTrue`, `empty`). A `{ from, to }` object for `between`.

createdChildNodes.description: Default child nodes created along with the requested node. BranchNode creation returns two AudienceFilter children. ExperimentBranchNode creation returns two regular VariantNode children and one control VariantNode.

ContactMailingLists.description: Mailing lists the contact is subscribed to, represented by key-value pairs of mailing list IDs and `true`.

EventRequestEventProperties.description: An object containing event property data for the event, available in emails sent by the event.

TransactionalRequestDataVariables.description:
An object containing data as defined by the data variables added to the transactional email template. Values can be of type string or number.

If you have added optional data variables to your email, you can exclude them from the dataVariables object or set the value to "".

If you have added an array data variable to your email, make sure to include an array matching the data variables you added to your array block.

filename.description: The name of the file, shown in email clients.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file.

data.description: The base64-encoded content of the file.

CreateAudienceSegmentRequestFilter.description: A tree of audience conditions combined with `match`.

SimplifiedWorkflowNodes.description: A map of node IDs to simplified node objects. Each node includes `typeName` and `nextNodeIds`, plus type-specific fields when present. To get the full node object, use the `GET /v1/workflows/{workflowId}/nodes/{nodeId}` endpoint.

UpdateEmailMessageRequestContactProperti.description: "Fallback values for contact properties, keyed by property name. Per-key merge: a string value sets the fallback, a null value deletes it, and keys omitted from the map are left unchanged."

UpdateEmailMessageRequestEventProperties.description: "Fallback values for event properties, keyed by property name. Per-key merge: a string value sets the fallback, a null value deletes it, and keys omitted from the map are left unchanged."

UpdateEmailMessageRequestDataVariablesFa.description: "Fallback values for data variables, keyed by variable name. Per-key merge: a string value sets the fallback, a null value deletes it, and keys omitted from the map are left unchanged."

EmailMessageResponseContactPropertiesFal.description: Fallback values for contact properties. Only present when set.

EmailMessageResponseEventPropertiesFallb.description: Fallback values for event properties. Only present when set.

EmailMessageResponseDataVariablesFallbac.description: Fallback values for data variables. Only present when set.

EmailMessagePreviewRequestContactPropert.description: Contact property values to render. Accepted for campaign and workflow previews.

EmailMessagePreviewRequestEventPropertie.description: Event property values to render. Accepted for workflow previews only.

EmailMessagePreviewRequestDataVariables.description: Transactional data variables to render. Accepted for transactional previews only.

label.description: A human-readable label for the item (for example, link text or a property name).

codeName.description: Machine-readable identifier when the rule refers to a property or variable, when applicable.

teamName.description: The name of the team the API key belongs to.

GetV1DedicatedSendingIps200Response.description: Returns an array of IP address strings.

ExamplePayload.description: Placeholder schema for path example refs

GetV1DedicatedSendingIps200ResponseRespo.description: Returns an array of IP address strings.

ContactFieldsMailingLists.description:
Manage mailing list subscriptions.

Include key-value pairs of mailing list IDs and a `boolean` denoting if the contact should be added (`true`) or removed (`false`) from the list.

ContactRequestMailingLists.description:
Manage mailing list subscriptions.

Include key-value pairs of mailing list IDs and a `boolean` denoting if the contact should be added (`true`) or removed (`false`) from the list.

Manage mailing list subscriptions.

EventRequestMailingLists.description:
Manage mailing list subscriptions.

Include key-value pairs of mailing list IDs and a `boolean` denoting if the contact should be added (`true`) or removed (`false`) from the list.

TransactionalRequestDataVariablesPropert.description:
An object containing data as defined by the data variables added to the transactional email template. Values can be of type string or number.

If you have added optional data variables to your email, you can exclude them from the dataVariables object or set the value to "".

If you have added an array data variable to your email, make sure to include an array matching the data variables you added to your array block.

SimplifiedWorkflowNodesProperty.description: A map of node IDs to simplified node objects. Each node includes `typeName` and `nextNodeIds`, plus type-specific fields when present. To get the full node object, use the `GET /v1/workflows/{workflowId}/nodes/{nodeId}` endpoint.

ContactOptInStatus.description: Double opt-in status. This will be `null` for contacts unless they are created via a form while double opt-in is enabled.

CreateCampaignResponseStatus.description: The status of the campaign (always `Draft` for new campaigns).

EventPatternSummaryIncomingWebhookPlatfo.description: The platform that sent this event pattern, if the event pattern is from an incoming webhook. Will be `null` for custom events.

EventPatternIncomingWebhookPlatform.description: The platform that sent this event pattern, if the event pattern is from an incoming webhook. Will be `null` for custom events.

WorkflowContactPropertyComparisonOperato.description: The comparison operator. It must be valid for the selected contact property's type and for the `is` or `was` side of the comparison. Number and boolean properties allow `empty` on `was`, but not on `is`.

UpdateEmailMessageRequestEmailFormat.description: The rendering format of the email.

EmailMessageResponseEmailFormat.description: The rendering format of the email.

GuardianRuleRule.description: The identifier of the Guardian rule that fired.
